volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 3.1.808 - Section 3.3.885)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 3.1.808
sections.original: Diceam .
sections.translation: Glycera .

sections.label: Section 3.1.808b
sections.original: Nempe eandem quae dudum constitutast .
sections.translation: The same , you mean , that was agreed upon a little time since .

sections.label: Section 3.1.808c
sections.original: Pax , abi .
sections.translation: Hush !— Be off .

sections.label: Section 3.1.809
sections.original: Meminero . sed quid meminisse id refert , rogo ego te tamen .
sections.translation: I ’ ll remember ; but still I don ’ t know what use it is to keep it in my mind .

sections.label: Section 3.1.810
sections.original: Ego enim dicam tum quando usus poscet ; interea tace ,
sections.translation: But I will tell you , at the time , when occasion shall require . Meanwhile , be quiet ; so that , bye and bye , when he too shall be acting his part [*] , you may , on the instant , be minding your cue .

sections.label: Section 3.1.812
sections.original: Ego eo intro igitur . —
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in then .

sections.label: Section 3.1.812b
sections.original: Et praecepta sobrie ut cures face .
sections.translation: Go , and do take care steadily to follow my instructions .

sections.label: Section 3.2.813
sections.original: quantas res turbo , quantas moveo machinas .
sections.translation: What mighty turmoils I create ! What mighty engines I do set to work ! This very day I shall take his mistress away from the Captain ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.815
sections.original: si centuriati bene sunt maniplares mei .
sections.translation: if my soldiers are only well drilled [*] . But I ’ ll call him out . ( Goes to the door and calls .) Hallo ! Sceledrus , if you are not busy , come out to the front of the house ; I , Palaestrio , call you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.818
sections.original: Non operaest Sceledro .
sections.translation: Sceledrus is not at leisure .

sections.label: Section 3.2.818b
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.818c
sections.original: Sorbet dormiens .
sections.translation: He ’ s fast asleep , gulping [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.819
sections.original: Quid , sorbet ?
sections.translation: How , gulping ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.819b
sections.original: Illud , stertit , volui dicere .
sections.translation: He ’ s snoring , ’ twas that I meant to say :

sections.label: Section 3.2.820
sections.original: sed quia consimile est , quom stertas , quasi sorbeas —
sections.translation: but , because ’ tis very like gulping when you are snoring — ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.2.821
sections.original: Eho an dórmit Sceledrus intus ?
sections.translation: What ! Is Sceledrus asleep in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.821b
sections.original: Non naso quidem ,
sections.translation: Not with his nose , in fact ; for with that he is calling out loud enough .

sections.label: Section 3.2.823
sections.original: dum misit nardum in amphoram , cellarius .
sections.translation: He has taken a cup by stealth ; [*] the butler has lately tapped a cask of nardine [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.824_825
sections.original: Eho tu sceleste , qui illi suppromus essuppromu's , eho —
sections.translation: Oho ! you rascal , you are his deputy-butler [*] . Oho !

sections.label: Section 3.2.826
sections.original: Quid vis ?
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.826b
sections.original: Qui lubitum est illi condormiscere ?
sections.translation: How has he thought fit to go to sleep ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.827
sections.original: Oculis opinor .
sections.translation: With his eyes , I suppose .

sections.label: Section 3.2.827b
sections.original: Non te istuc rogito , scelus .
sections.translation: I don ’ t ask you that , you vagabond . Step this way : you ’ re undone now , unless I know the truth . Did you draw the wine for him ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.829b
sections.original: Non prompsi .
sections.translation: I did not draw it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.829c
sections.original: Negas ?
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.830
sections.original: Nego hercle vero , nam ille me vetuit dicere ;
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do deny it undoubtedly ; for he charged me not to tell . I really didn ’ t just [*] draw for him eight half pints into a pitcher , and , when drawn , he didn ’ t just drink it hot [*] , at his breakfast .

sections.label: Section 3.2.833
sections.original: Neque tu bibisti ?
sections.translation: And you didn ’ t just drink as well ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.833b
sections.original: Di me perdant , si bibi ,
sections.translation: The Gods confound me if I did drink — if I could drink .

sections.label: Section 3.2.834b
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.834c
sections.original: Quia enim obsorbui ;
sections.translation: Because , in fact , I only sipped ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.835
sections.original: nam nimis calebat , amburebat gutturem .
sections.translation: for it was too hot ; it burnt my throat .

sections.label: Section 3.2.836
sections.original: Alii ebrii sunt , alii poscam potitant .
sections.translation: Some are gloriously drunk , while others are drinking vinegar-water [*] . The cellar ’ s trusted to an honest butler , as well as under-butler .

sections.label: Section 3.2.838
sections.original: Tu hércle idem fáceres , si tibi esset credita :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you ’ d be doing the same , if it was entrusted to you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.839_840
sections.original: quoniam aemulari non licet , nunc invides .
sections.translation: Since you can ’ t follow our example , you are envious now .

sections.label: Section 3.2.841
sections.original: Eho an umquam prompsit antehac ? responde , scelus .
sections.translation: Come , now , did he ever draw any wine before this ? Answer me , you rascal . And , that you may understand it , I give you this notice : if you purposely tell me an untruth , you shall be put to the torture .

sections.label: Section 3.2.844
sections.original: Ita vero ? ut tu ipse me dixisse delices ,
sections.translation: Indeed so ? That you may inform , forsooth , that I told you ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.845
sections.original: post e sagina ego eiciar cellaria ,
sections.translation: and then I shall be turned out of my fattening post in the cellar , that you may find another under-butler to draw for your own self .

sections.label: Section 3.2.847
sections.original: Non edepol faciam . age eloquere audacter mihi .
sections.translation: On my honour , I will not ; come , speak out boldly to me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.848
sections.original: Numquam edepol vidi promere . verum hoc erat :
sections.translation: By my troth , I never saw him draw any . But thus was it ; he requested me , and then I drew it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.850
sections.original: Hoc illi crebro capite sistebant cadi .
sections.translation: Think of that now ! very frequently , I guess , the casks were standing on their heads [*] there .

sections.label: Section 3.2.851
sections.original: Non hercle tam istoc valide cassabant cadi ;
sections.translation: No , faith , the casks would not [*] have stood so very badly there . But there happened to be in the cellar a bit of a slippery spot ; a two-pint pot was placed there , near the casks , in this fashion ( shows the way .) Frequently , that was filled ten times in a day .

sections.label: Section 3.2.856
sections.original: ubi bacchabatur aula , cassabant cadi .
sections.translation: When the pot acted the reveller , the casks were all tottering .

sections.label: Section 3.2.857
sections.original: Abi , abi íntro iam . vos in cella vinaria
sections.translation: Get you gone in-doors . Both of you , I find , are acting the revellers in the wine-cellar . I ’ faith , I shall fetch my master home just now from the Forum .

sections.label: Section 3.2.859
sections.original: Perii , excruciabit mé erus , domum si venerit ,
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined . My master , when he comes home , will have me tortured ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.860
sections.original: quom haec facta scibit , quia sibi non dixerim .
sections.translation: when he knows of these doings . I ’ faith , I ’ ll fly somewhither , and put off this punishment to another day . ( To the AUDIENCE .) don ’ t you tell him [*] , I do entreat you most earnestly .

sections.label: Section 3.2.863
sections.original: Quo té agis ?
sections.translation: Whither are you betaking yourself ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.863b
sections.original: Missus sum aliquo : iam huc revenero .
sections.translation: I am sent elsewhere : I ’ ll come back here just now .

sections.label: Section 3.2.864
sections.original: Quis misit ?
sections.translation: Who has sent you ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.864b
sections.original: Philocomasium .
sections.translation: Philocomasium .

sections.label: Section 3.2.864c
sections.original: Abi , actutum redi .
sections.translation: Go ; be back directly .

sections.label: Section 3.2.865
sections.original: Quaeso tamen tu meam partem , infortunium
sections.translation: If it is divided , prithee do you only take my share of the punishment while I ’ m away .

sections.label: Section 3.2.867
sections.original: Modo intellexi quam rem mulier gesserit :
sections.translation: So — I understand what scheme the lady is upon . Because Sceledrus is asleep , she has sent her under-keeper away out of doors , whilst she may pass from our house to next door . That ’ s all right .

sections.label: Section 3.2.870
sections.original: sed Periplectomenus quam ei mandavi mulierem
sections.translation: ( Looks down the street .) But Periplecomenus is bringing here a woman of very comely appearance , her , for whom I commissioned him . By my faith , the Gods are helping us in this matter . How becomingly drest she struts along [*] , not like a Courtesan . This business is prospering charmingly in our hands .

sections.label: Section 3.3.874
sections.original: Rem omném tibi , Acroteleútium , tibique úna , Milphidíppa ,
sections.translation: I have explained the whole affair , Acroteleutium , to you , and , Milphidippa , to you as well .

sections.label: Section 3.3.875
sections.original: domi démonstravi in ordine . hanc fabricam fallaciasque
sections.translation: If you don ’ t well understand this device and plan , I wish you to hear it all over again . If you comprehend it aright , there is something else that we may speak of in preference .

sections.label: Section 3.3.878
sections.original: Stultitia atque insipientia mea istaec sit , mi patrone ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it would be folly , and ignorance , and foolishness , for me to engage in the service of another , or to promise you my assistance ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.880
sections.original: si ea in ópificina nesciam aut mala esse aut fraudulenta .
sections.translation: if , in its fabrication , I did not know how to be either mischievous or clever at deceiving .

sections.label: Section 3.3.881
sections.original: At melius est monerier .
sections.translation: But , ’ tis better for you to be instructed .

sections.label: Section 3.3.881b
sections.original: Meretricem commoneri ,
sections.translation: Really I don ’ t understand of what great use it is for a Courtesan to be instructed . How now ! have I told you all in vain , after my ears had drunk in the draughts [*] of your discourse , in what fashion it was possible for the Captain to be cajoled ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.885
sections.original: At nemo solus satis sapit . nam ego multos saepe vidi
sections.translation: But no one , unaided , is sufficiently perfect ; for full oft have I seen many a person lose the road to good advice before they had found it .
